See [docs/AGENT-TEAMS-GUIDE.md](docs/AGENT-TEAMS-GUIDE.md) for the full Agent Teams usage guide. ## How It Works ### The Lifecycle ``` IDEA → /init (configure preferences) → /brainstorm (explore requirements) ← interactive dialogue → OR /import-spec (from spec-kit artifacts) ← spec-driven alternative → OR /import-spec-file (handwritten spec) ← existing doc alternative → /plan (research + decompose) → /build (parallel execute + review) → repeat plan→build per phase → /ship (verify + deliver) → SHIPPED ``` ### State Management Shipyard uses a dual state system: - **File state** (`.shipyard/` directory): Cross-session persistence for project vision, roadmap, plans, and progress. Survives session restarts and can be committed to git. Includes backup-on-write (`.bak`), SHA-256 checksums, and working notes (`NOTES.md`) for compaction resilience. - **Native tasks** (TaskCreate/TaskUpdate): In-session UI visibility showing real-time progress of phases and plans. ### Key Design Principles - **Fresh context per task**: Each builder agent runs in a clean 200k-token context, preventing quality degradation - **Two-stage review + security audit**: Spec compliance and code quality per task, comprehensive security audit per phase - **Configurable gates**: Security audit, simplification review, and IaC validation can be toggled in `config.json` or skipped with `--light`. Hook kill switch (`SHIPYARD_DISABLE_HOOKS`) available for debugging - **Code simplification**: Post-phase analysis catches AI-generated duplication and bloat across tasks - **Documentation gen

subagents.name: academic-research-synthesizer
subagents.description: Synthesize academic research with proper citations and evidence chains.
subagents.content: You are an expert research assistant specializing in academic synthesis. Given a research question, you search for relevant papers, synthesize findings, and produce well-cited summaries with proper evidence chains and academic rigor.

subagents.name: code-reviewer
subagents.description: Perform thorough code reviews with actionable feedback on quality, security, and maintainability.
subagents.content: You are an expert code reviewer. Analyze code for bugs, security vulnerabilities, performance issues, and style violations. Provide clear, actionable feedback with specific line references and suggested improvements.

subagents.name: data-analyst
subagents.description: Analyze datasets, generate insights, and produce visualizations and reports.
subagents.content: You are an expert data analyst. Given a dataset or data file, explore its structure, identify patterns and anomalies, generate statistical summaries, and produce clear reports with actionable insights.

subagents.name: security-auditor
subagents.description: Audit codebases for security vulnerabilities, OWASP issues, and compliance concerns.
subagents.content: You are a security expert specializing in application security audits. Identify OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, injection flaws, authentication issues, and data exposure risks. Provide severity ratings and remediation guidance.

subagents.name: ui-designer
subagents.description: Design and implement accessible, visually polished UI components following modern design principles.
subagents.content: You are an expert UI/UX designer and frontend developer. Create beautiful, accessible components following WCAG 2.1 AA standards. Focus on mobile-first responsive design, semantic HTML, and modern CSS patterns.

skills.name: docs-writer
skills.description: Generate clear, accurate technical documentation from code and context.
skills.content: Create comprehensive documentation including README files, API references, inline comments, and usage examples. Write for the intended audience with appropriate technical depth and clarity.

skills.name: performance-audit
skills.description: Identify and fix performance bottlenecks in code and applications.
skills.content: Analyze code for performance issues: inefficient algorithms, unnecessary re-renders, N+1 queries, memory leaks, and bundle size problems. Provide concrete fixes with measured impact.

skills.name: refactor
skills.description: Systematically refactor code for improved quality, readability, and maintainability.
skills.content: Review code for refactoring opportunities. Focus on: extract functions, reduce duplication, improve naming, simplify logic, apply SOLID principles, and reduce cognitive complexity.

skills.name: security-review
skills.description: Review code changes for security vulnerabilities before merge.
skills.content: Perform security-focused code review. Check for injection vulnerabilities, authentication flaws, authorization bypasses, sensitive data exposure, and insecure dependencies.

skills.name: test-writer
skills.description: Write comprehensive unit, integration, and E2E tests with high coverage.
skills.content: Write thorough tests covering happy paths, edge cases, and error conditions. Follow testing best practices: descriptive names, AAA pattern, minimal mocking, and meaningful assertions.

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Explain code or concepts clearly with practical examples and analogies.
commands.content: Provide a clear, concise explanation with practical examples. Adapt the technical depth to the apparent expertise level of the question. Use analogies where helpful.

commands.name: fix
commands.description: Diagnose and fix bugs, errors, and issues in code.
commands.content: Analyze the error or bug, identify the root cause, and provide a working fix. Explain what was wrong and why the fix works.

commands.name: optimize
commands.description: Optimize code for performance, bundle size, or resource usage.
commands.content: Analyze and optimize the specified code for better performance. Identify bottlenecks, suggest algorithmic improvements, and reduce unnecessary operations.

commands.name: review
commands.description: Perform a comprehensive code review with actionable feedback.
commands.content: Review code for correctness, maintainability, performance, security, and style. Provide specific, actionable feedback with examples and suggested improvements.

commands.name: test
commands.description: Generate tests for the specified code or feature.
commands.content: Write comprehensive tests covering the specified code. Include unit tests for individual functions, integration tests for interactions, and edge case coverage.

hooks.name: format-on-save
hooks.description: Automatically format code files after they are edited or written.
hooks.content: Run prettier or language-specific formatter after file edits to maintain consistent code style.

hooks.name: git-status
hooks.description: Show git status after file changes to track what has been modified.
hooks.content: Display a compact git status summary after file operations to keep the developer informed of pending changes and staged files.

hooks.name: lint-check
hooks.description: Run linting checks after code edits to catch issues early.
hooks.content: Execute ESLint, Pylint, or other language-specific linters after file modifications to enforce code style rules and catch potential bugs.

hooks.name: log-commands
hooks.description: Log all executed commands and tool invocations to an audit file.
hooks.content: Record all bash commands executed during a Claude Code session to a timestamped audit log for debugging and compliance purposes.

hooks.name: test-runner
hooks.description: Automatically run relevant tests after code changes.
hooks.content: Detect changed files and run the associated test suite automatically after code modifications to catch regressions immediately.
